prereq_check.title: Checking your Mac
prereq_check.subtitle: macOS, Apple Silicon, RAM, disk
prereq_check.why: Ostler runs entirely on this machine. We confirm it has the muscle for local AI before we touch anything.

setup_questions.title: A few questions
setup_questions.subtitle: Two minutes, then you can walk away
setup_questions.why: Your name, your country, your timezone, your assistant's name. These shape everything Ostler does later, but you only answer them once.

homebrew_install.title: Installing Homebrew + system tools
homebrew_install.subtitle: The package layer Ostler uses
homebrew_install.why: Homebrew is the standard macOS package manager. Ostler uses it to fetch Docker, Ollama, and a few CLI tools. Nothing system-wide that conflicts with what you already have.
homebrew_install.long_running_copy: First-time Homebrew install can take a few minutes on slow networks.

docker_install.title: Starting Docker
docker_install.subtitle: Container runtime for the data pipeline
docker_install.why: Ostler runs its databases (Qdrant, Oxigraph, Redis) and wiki in Docker. Ollama and the assistant binary run natively – Docker is only the data layer.

ollama_install.title: Setting up Ollama
ollama_install.subtitle: Local AI engine (no cloud)
ollama_install.why: Ollama runs language models on your Mac's Apple Silicon. Every prompt your assistant answers, every wiki summary, runs through this. Nothing leaves the machine.

config_save.title: Saving your configuration
config_save.subtitle: Names, timezone, channel choices
config_save.why: Writes your answers to ~/.ostler/config/ so future runs (and the assistant) pick them up without asking again.

encrypt_db.title: Encrypting your databases
encrypt_db.subtitle: PBKDF2 + SQLCipher + recovery key
encrypt_db.why: Your knowledge graph is encrypted at rest with the passphrase you chose. The recovery key is shown once – write it down. We cannot help if both are lost.

fda_extract.title: Reading your Mac's databases
fda_extract.subtitle: The instant onboarding bit
fda_extract.why: Ostler reads Safari, iMessage, Notes, Calendar, Photos, Reminders, and Mail directly from macOS. Nothing leaves your machine. Full Disk Access is a one-time grant in System Settings.
fda_extract.long_running_copy: First-run extraction can take a couple of minutes if your Mail or Photos library is large.

graph_db_start.title: Starting the knowledge graph
graph_db_start.subtitle: Qdrant + Oxigraph + Redis
graph_db_start.why: Three small databases boot up: vectors for semantic search, RDF triples for relationships, Redis for the message bus. All local, all yours.

ai_models.title: Downloading AI models
ai_models.subtitle: This is the big one (~7GB)
ai_models.why: The assistant model and the embedding model. One large download, then nothing more from the cloud. If your connection drops we retry three times.
ai_models.long_running_copy: On a slow connection this can take 15-20 minutes. You can leave the installer running.

import_pipeline.title: Installing the data import pipeline
import_pipeline.subtitle: Parsers for 20+ platforms
import_pipeline.why: The pipeline parses the data sources you've consented to: GDPR exports, Apple Mail, iCloud calendars, browser history. Each parser is local Python, no cloud.

import_data.title: Importing your data
import_data.subtitle: Building your knowledge graph
import_data.why: First-pass import: contacts, conversations, calendar events, Safari history. The wiki compiler runs after this, turning the graph into pages you can read.
import_data.long_running_copy: Import time scales with your history. Years of iMessage data can take 10-20 minutes.

doctor_setup.title: Setting up Ostler Doctor
doctor_setup.subtitle: Diagnostic dashboard
doctor_setup.why: Doctor is the local-only health dashboard. If something stops working, Doctor tells you what and why. No telemetry, no cloud – diagnostics stay on your Mac.

hub_power.title: Hub power policy
hub_power.subtitle: MacBook battery-aware mode
hub_power.why: On a MacBook Hub, Docker and Ollama pause when you unplug and resume when you plug in, so your battery life stays roughly normal. On a Mac Mini, this is a no-op.

email_ingest.title: Email ingest
email_ingest.subtitle: Hourly Apple Mail drain
email_ingest.why: Once an hour, Ostler drains new mail from Apple Mail's local store into the graph. Apple does the auth (IMAP/OAuth), Ostler reads the local copy. Zero credentials held by us.

wiki_recompile_agent.title: Wiki rebuild scheduler
wiki_recompile_agent.subtitle: Daily knowledge compilation
wiki_recompile_agent.why: Once a day (default 4am) the wiki compiler reruns over fresh data. Karpathy-style: facts in, knowledge out. You can also trigger it on demand later.

ostler_assistant.title: Installing the assistant
ostler_assistant.subtitle: Your local AI agent
ostler_assistant.why: The Rust binary that drives iMessage, email, and tool use. Lives at ~/.ostler/bin and runs as a launchd agent. Everything it can do is gated by the channels and permissions you set.

wiki_compile.title: Compiling your wiki
wiki_compile.subtitle: First-run knowledge build
wiki_compile.why: Turns the imported graph into a browsable wiki at localhost:8044. Pages: people, places, organisations, threads, topics. Each one is a flat MkDocs page you can also edit by hand.
wiki_compile.long_running_copy: First compile crunches everything from scratch. Subsequent runs are incremental and much faster.

health_check.title: Health check
health_check.subtitle: Confirming everything is up
health_check.why: Quick sanity pass – every container running, every LaunchAgent loaded, the wiki responding. If anything is off, the report tells you exactly what.
